description:
Add two schema changes required by the at-risk review feature and the push notification reminder, then run a Prisma migration.

CHANGE 1 — NotificationType enum (prisma/schema.prisma)
Add REVIEW_REMINDER to the existing enum so the daily cron can record that a reminder was sent and the dedup query can filter on it:

  enum NotificationType {
    ITEM_EXPIRING
    ITEM_EXPIRED
    ITEM_STATUS_CHANGED
    REVIEW_REMINDER   // new
    SYSTEM
  }

CHANGE 2 — User model (prisma/schema.prisma)
Add a nullable lastReviewedAt DateTime field so the server can track when a user last completed a review session. This is the cross-device source of truth; localStorage is only a fast-path cache:

  model User {
    ...
    lastReviewedAt DateTime?
    ...
  }

CHANGE 3 — userPreferencesSchema (src/types/user-preferences.ts)
Add two new preference keys used by the notification settings UI and the cron:

  reviewReminderEnabled: z.boolean().default(true),
  reviewReminderTime: z.string().regex(/^\d{2}:\d{2}$/).default('09:00'),

The reviewReminderTime field is added now so the schema is stable, but the time-picker UI is out of scope for this issue — just the schema addition and default.

MIGRATION
Run: npx prisma migrate dev --name add-review-reminder-schema
Verify migration file is generated and committed.
title: Schema changes: REVIEW_REMINDER notification type + lastReviewedAt on User
useAtRiskReview + useAtRiskReviewSession hooks

description: Epic covering the full mobile swipe-to-review card deck experience for at-risk inventory items (BAD, OLD, FRESH expiring within 3 days), plus a daily push notification reminder that deep-links into the deck. Users are prompted once per day on mobile to swipe through at-risk items and log whether each was eaten, discarded, or partially consumed — similar to how Tinder lets you review profiles via a swipable card stack. The feature is gated to mobile (isMobile === true) and fires at most once per calendar day via a localStorage fast-path cache backed by a lastReviewedAt DB field. All required dependencies (framer-motion, vaul, canvas-confetti, nuqs, web-push) are already installed. Implementation plan: AT_RISK_REVIEW_PLAN.md
title: At-risk items review experience

description:
Add a 'Daily Review Reminder' sub-section to the existing SettingsTabNotifications component so users can opt out of the daily push nudge without fully disabling all push notifications.

FILE: src/components/settings/SettingsTabNotifications.tsx

CHANGES:
Add a new Card below the existing Push Notifications card. Only render this card when isSubscribed === true (no point showing reminder settings if push is not enabled).

UI DESIGN:

  <Card>
    <CardHeader>
      <CardTitle className='flex items-center gap-2'>
        <CalendarClock className='h-5 w-5' />
        Daily Review Reminder
      </CardTitle>
      <CardDescription>
        Get a daily nudge when you have items that need attention.
        Sent once per day at 9 AM.
      </CardDescription>
    </CardHeader>
    <CardContent>
      <UserSettingSwitchCard
        settingKey='reviewReminderEnabled'
        label='Enable daily reminder'
        description='We will send you a push notification when you have BAD, OLD, or soon-to-expire items to review.'
      />
    </CardContent>
  </Card>

The UserSettingSwitchCard component (src/components/UserSettingSwitchCard.tsx) already handles reading and writing user preferences via the existing user.updatePreference tRPC mutation — just pass the settingKey and it wires itself up. The reviewReminderEnabled key was added to userPreferencesSchema in the schema issue.

Import CalendarClock from lucide-react (already installed).

FUTURE NOTE: A time-picker for reviewReminderTime can be added here later without restructuring. Leave a TODO comment: 'TODO: Add time picker for reviewReminderTime preference once UI pattern is decided.'

DEPENDS ON: Schema changes (broccoli-dashboard-0cr) for the reviewReminderEnabled preference key.
title: Settings: Daily Review Reminder toggle in SettingsTabNotifications

description:
Build the deck component that renders the stacked card queue and manages AnimatePresence transitions between cards. Lives at src/components/core/AtRiskReview/AtRiskReviewDeck.tsx.

PROPS:
  interface AtRiskReviewDeckProps {
    queue: CombinedItemType[];
    onDecision: (itemId: number, decision: ReviewDecision) => void;
  }

STACKING LAYOUT:
Render the top 3 items from queue simultaneously (or fewer if queue.length < 3), each as an absolutely-positioned AtRiskReviewCard. The container div needs position: relative with a fixed height tall enough for the largest possible card (use min-h-[520px] or similar).

Positioning per stack depth (index 0 = top/front):
  index 0 (top):    scale=1.00, translateY=0px,  zIndex=3, isTop=true
  index 1 (middle): scale=0.96, translateY=8px,  zIndex=2, isTop=false
  index 2 (back):   scale=0.92, translateY=16px, zIndex=1, isTop=false

Pass these as initial/animate props on each card's wrapper motion.div so they animate smoothly when the top card is removed and the stack 'shuffles up'.

ANIMATEPRESENCE:
Wrap the card list in <AnimatePresence mode='popLayout'> keyed by item.id. When the top card is removed from queue (after onDecision fires), AnimatePresence handles the exit animation (card already flew off-screen via AtRiskReviewCard's own animation) and the remaining cards animate into their new depth positions via their layout prop.

EMPTY STATE: When queue.length === 0, render null (the parent AtRiskReviewModal will switch to the AtRiskReviewComplete screen).

SKELETON STATE: When isLoading is true (passed as prop), render a single skeleton card placeholder — a rounded-2xl with animate-pulse bg-muted at the same dimensions as a card.

GESTURE HINT: On first render (or if no swipe has been detected after 2 seconds), show a subtle animated hint — a small arrow/swipe icon that animates left and right to guide the user. Dismiss permanently once first swipe is detected. Store dismissal in localStorage key 'broccoli:atRiskReview:hintSeen'.

DEPENDS ON: AtRiskReviewCard (broccoli-dashboard-z4y), AtRiskReviewCardOverlay (broccoli-dashboard-rqy), AtRiskReviewCardDetails (broccoli-dashboard-le3).
title: AtRiskReviewDeck: stacked card deck orchestrator
Service worker: deep-link AT_RISK_REVIEW notification to card deck

description:
Wire the completed AtRiskReview feature into the app and add the final polish layer. This is the last front-end task. FILE: src/app/(app)/(scrollable)/app/page.tsx Mount AtRiskReviewModal as the first child of the page, before the header div. It self-manages its open/close state so no props are needed: import { AtRiskReviewModal } from '@/components/core/AtRiskReview'; // Inside AppPage return:
... existing page content ...
Also add index.ts at src/components/core/AtRiskReview/index.ts re-exporting all public components and hooks. ACCESSIBILITY: - Add keyboard navigation to AtRiskReviewDeck: useHotkeys (react-hotkeys-hook, already installed) binding ArrowRight → onDecision('eaten'), ArrowLeft → onDecision('discarded'), ArrowUp → onDecision('skipped'), Escape → close modal. Only active when the deck is open and isTop card is mounted. - All card interactive elements (drag area, buttons) must have aria-label attributes. Drag area: aria-label='Drag card: swipe right if eaten, left to discard'. Eaten button: aria-label='Mark as eaten'. Discard button: aria-label='Mark as discarded'. - AtRiskReviewModal Drawer.Title should read 'Kitchen check-in: review {count} at-risk items'. - Focus management: when modal opens, focus the first action button on the top card. When modal closes, return focus to the element that was focused before. HAPTIC FEEDBACK: - In AtRiskReviewCard's handleDragEnd, after determining the decision type and BEFORE calling onDecision, call: if ('vibrate' in navigator) navigator.vibrate(50); - For button taps (Eaten/Discard buttons), same pattern — vibrate on click. - Do not vibrate for 'skipped' — that is intentional and passive. DARK MODE REVIEW: - Walk through all status colors in both themes. Confirm: - Red-400 border + red-950/30 bg works in dark - Yellow-400 border + yellow-950/30 bg works in dark - Orange-400 border + orange-950/30 bg works in dark - Green/Red overlays are visible but not garish in dark mode - The Vaul Drawer uses dark:bg-zinc-900 (consistent with existing BottomDrawer) EDGE CASES TO VERIFY: - 0 items: modal never mounts (gate in useAtRiskReview ensures isEmpty=true from the start) - 1 item: single card with no stack effect (AtRiskReviewDeck handles queue.length < 3 gracefully) - Slow network: skeleton card shows while query is loading - User closes mid-deck and reopens app same day: modal re-opens with remaining items (localStorage not marked complete, session not marked complete) - User closes mid-deck and reopens app next day: modal opens fresh with full new at-risk list DEPENDS ON: AtRiskReviewModal (broccoli-dashboard-n5z), AtRiskReviewComplete (broccoli-dashboard-ehp).
title: AppPage integration: mount AtRiskReviewModal + accessibility + haptics
AtRiskReviewComplete: session summary screen with confetti
Cron: send-review-reminders daily push notification script
tRPC: item.getAtRiskItems query + user.markReviewComplete mutation

description:
Build the expandable detail panel that slides open when the user taps 'Update details' on a card. Lives at src/components/core/AtRiskReview/AtRiskReviewCardDetails.tsx. This replaces having to open the full UpdateItemForm dialog — it brings the most important controls inline on the card.

PROPS:
  interface AtRiskReviewCardDetailsProps {
    item: CombinedItemType;
    onConfirm: (decision: { type: 'updated'; percentConsumed: number; status?: ItemStatusType }) => void;
    onCancel: () => void;
  }

LOCAL STATE:
  - percentConsumed: number (initialized from item.percentConsumed)
  - status: ItemStatusType | undefined (initialized as undefined — 'in progress')

ANIMATION: Wrap with <motion.div layout animate={{ height: 'auto' }} initial={{ height: 0 }} exit={{ height: 0 }} transition={{ type: 'spring', bounce: 0.1 }}> inside an AnimatePresence block in the parent card.

UI LAYOUT (mirrors UpdateItemForm design — reuse its visual patterns):
  1. Large percentage display: centered, text-5xl font-bold tabular-nums showing '{percentConsumed}%'
  2. Native range slider: input type=range 0-100, styled with accent-primary
  3. Quick-pick buttons grid (5 columns): [0%, 25%, 50%, 75%, 100%] — highlighted when selected (variant='default' vs 'outline')
  4. Status toggle row: two large buttons side-by-side
       [🍽 Still eating]  [✅ All eaten]  [🗑 Threw it out]
     - 'Still eating' clears status override (stays as item's current status with updated %)
     - 'All eaten' sets status=EATEN and percentConsumed=100
     - 'Threw it out' sets status=DISCARDED
  5. Confirm button: full-width, green, 'Save & Next →' — calls onConfirm with current state
  6. Cancel link: small text button 'Never mind' — calls onCancel to collapse the panel

SMART DEFAULTS: If percentConsumed reaches 100 via the slider or quick-pick buttons, automatically highlight 'All eaten'. If status is set to DISCARDED, the % is visually irrelevant (grey it out).

REUSE: The % display and quick-pick button pattern are already implemented in UpdateItemForm — copy the JSX pattern from src/components/core/ItemForm.tsx UpdateItemForm component.

DEPENDS ON: useAtRiskReview hook (broccoli-dashboard-479), should be built alongside AtRiskReviewCard (broccoli-dashboard-z4y).
title: AtRiskReviewCardDetails: expandable inline % consumed + status panel

description:
Build the top-level modal shell that wraps the card deck experience. Lives at src/components/core/AtRiskReview/AtRiskReviewModal.tsx. This is the entry point component mounted in AppPage.

RESPONSIBILITIES:
1. Gate logic — uses useAtRiskReviewSession to determine if the modal should auto-open. Only opens when:
   a. isMobile === true (from useMediaQueries context)
   b. session.shouldShow === true (not reviewed today, or forceOpen is true)
   c. atRiskItems.length > 0 (from useAtRiskReview)
   If any condition fails, renders null.

2. Modal shell — wraps content in the existing BottomDrawer component (src/components/BottomDrawer.tsx) with renderHeader={null} (no default header — custom header is inlined). Alternatively, use Vaul Drawer.Root directly for more control. The drawer should be full-height on mobile (h-[100dvh]) so the deck has maximum vertical space.

3. Custom header (rendered inside drawer):
   - Left: 'Kitchen check-in' title + item count subtitle ('N items need attention')
   - Right: X icon button with tooltip 'Remind me later' — closes modal WITHOUT marking session complete (user will see it again)
   - Below header: 'Don't show today' small text link — closes AND calls session.markComplete()

4. Progress bar: thin (h-1) bar below the header showing reviewed/total ratio. Use the existing @radix-ui/react-progress (Progress component). Example: 3 of 7 reviewed → 43% filled. Animate width transitions smoothly.

5. Body: renders AtRiskReviewDeck (passing queue, onDecision, isLoading) when queue is non-empty, or AtRiskReviewComplete when queue is empty and results exist.

6. Push notification deep-link handling:
   - Read ?intent=review query param via useQueryState('intent') from nuqs
   - If intent === 'review': call session.setForceOpen(true), then clear the param: router.replace('/app', { scroll: false })
   - Add a useEffect listener on navigator.serviceWorker for message events: if event.data?.type === 'OPEN_AT_RISK_REVIEW', call session.setForceOpen(true)
   - Both paths force the modal open regardless of the localStorage gate

7. On modal close (Drawer onOpenChange to false):
   - If queue is exhausted (isEmpty): call session.markComplete()
   - If dismissed mid-deck: do NOT call markComplete — just close

VAUL DRAWER CONFIG:
  <Drawer.Root open={isOpen} onOpenChange={handleOpenChange}>
    Use the existing BottomDrawer or replicate its pattern. Set snapPoints if needed for full-height feel on mobile. The drawer should push/scale the background content slightly (existing BottomDrawer already does this with the framer-motion scale animation).

DEPENDS ON: AtRiskReviewDeck (broccoli-dashboard-8d4), useAtRiskReviewSession (broccoli-dashboard-479), AtRiskReviewComplete (built in same phase — coordinate).
title: AtRiskReviewModal: Vaul Drawer shell with once-per-day gate + progress bar

close_reason: Superseded by epic broccoli-dashboard-5ry with full issue breakdown
description: As a user on mobile I should be prompted when opening the app to review at risk items BAD, OLD, going to be OLD in the next 3 days via an attractive and intuitive card swiping system similar to how Tinder lets you review and take actions on profiles in a swipable card stack. This should only trigger once per day and should be focused with asking a user if they finished an item, and if not how much is left or did they throw it away, similar to the update item form. We want to streamline the item review/updating process. Implementation plan: AT_RISK_REVIEW_PLAN.md
title: Items at risk review experience

description:
Build the directional action hint overlays that appear on the card as the user drags. Lives at src/components/core/AtRiskReview/AtRiskReviewCardOverlay.tsx. This component is rendered inside AtRiskReviewCard and receives the dragX MotionValue as a prop so it can derive its own opacity reactively without re-renders.

PROPS:
  interface AtRiskReviewCardOverlayProps {
    dragX: MotionValue<number>;
    threshold: number;  // SWIPE_THRESHOLD constant from parent (80)
  }

IMPLEMENTATION:
  Two absolutely-positioned overlays, both covering the full card face (inset-0 absolute pointer-events-none rounded-2xl):

  RIGHT overlay (Eaten):
    const eatOpacity = useTransform(dragX, [0, threshold], [0, 1]);
    Background: bg-green-500/20 dark:bg-green-400/20
    Content: centered ✅ icon (Lucide Utensils, size 48) + bold 'EATEN' label
    Text color: text-green-700 dark:text-green-300
    Border: border-2 border-green-500/50 rounded-2xl

  LEFT overlay (Discard):
    const discardOpacity = useTransform(dragX, [-threshold, 0], [1, 0]);
    Background: bg-red-500/20 dark:bg-red-400/20
    Content: centered 🗑 icon (Lucide Trash2, size 48) + bold 'DISCARD' label
    Text color: text-red-700 dark:text-red-300
    Border: border-2 border-red-500/50 rounded-2xl

Both overlays use <motion.div style={{ opacity }} ...> so opacity is driven by the MotionValue directly (no React state, no re-renders). The overlays should be z-10 relative to card content so they render above the card body but below any absolutely-positioned buttons.

DESIGN NOTE: The opacity ramp starts at 0 and reaches 1 exactly at the SWIPE_THRESHOLD (80px). This gives a clear signal that the action will commit. Beyond threshold, opacity stays at 1 (useTransform clamps by default).

DEPENDS ON: AtRiskReviewCard (broccoli-dashboard-z4y).
title: AtRiskReviewCardOverlay: directional swipe hint overlays

description:
Build the core draggable card component at src/components/core/AtRiskReview/AtRiskReviewCard.tsx. This is the most complex component in the feature — get the physics and visual feedback right.

PROPS:
  interface AtRiskReviewCardProps {
    item: CombinedItemType;
    onDecision: (decision: ReviewDecision) => void;
    style?: MotionStyle;  // for deck positioning (scale, translateY, zIndex passed from AtRiskReviewDeck)
    isTop: boolean;       // only the top card is draggable
  }

FRAMER MOTION SETUP:
  const dragX = useMotionValue(0);
  const dragY = useMotionValue(0);
  const rotate = useTransform(dragX, [-200, 0, 200], [-15, 0, 15]);
  const SWIPE_THRESHOLD = 80;

  Drag config:
  - drag={isTop ? true : false}
  - dragConstraints={{ left: 0, right: 0, top: -100, bottom: 0 }} (elastic feel — card snaps back if below threshold)
  - dragElastic={0.7}
  - whileDrag={{ cursor: 'grabbing' }}

  handleDragEnd(_: PointerEvent, info: PanInfo):
  - if info.offset.x > SWIPE_THRESHOLD → onDecision({ type: 'eaten' })
  - if info.offset.x < -SWIPE_THRESHOLD → onDecision({ type: 'discarded' })
  - if info.offset.y < -SWIPE_THRESHOLD → onDecision({ type: 'skipped' })
  - else → animate back to { x: 0, y: 0, rotate: 0 } via spring

  On programmatic decision (button taps), animate the card off-screen first via animate(), THEN call onDecision in the animation completion callback so the parent doesn't remove it before the exit animation finishes.

CARD VISUAL STRUCTURE (Tailwind):
- Outer wrapper: rounded-2xl shadow-xl bg-white dark:bg-zinc-900 overflow-hidden w-full max-w-sm mx-auto
- Status header strip: colored top border (4px) and subtle tinted background:
    BAD → border-red-400, bg-red-50 dark:bg-red-950/30
    OLD → border-yellow-400, bg-yellow-50 dark:bg-yellow-950/30
    FRESH expiring → border-orange-400, bg-orange-50 dark:bg-orange-950/30
- Status badge + urgency string (top-left): reuse getItemStatusColor from ItemForm.tsx
- Urgency copy (top-right, muted):
    BAD → 'This has gone bad'
    OLD → 'Going bad soon'
    FRESH 0 days → 'Expires today!'
    FRESH 1 day → 'Expires tomorrow'
    FRESH 2-3 days → 'Expires in N days'
    Use getTimeRemainingString from src/utils/expiration.ts
- Item type icon: centered, large (64x64) using existing ImageFallback component with urlEndpoint and fallback
- Item name: text-2xl font-bold text-center
- Item details: text-sm text-muted-foreground text-center (description or 'quantity unit')
- % consumed bar: if percentConsumed > 0, show a simple progress bar with label
- Action buttons row: two large buttons — Discarded (left, red outline) and Eaten (right, green fill)
- 'Update details' chevron button at bottom center that toggles AtRiskReviewCardDetails (passed as children or via isExpanded state)

REDUCED MOTION: wrap rotation and fly-out animation in a check for window.matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)') — if true, skip rotate transform and use opacity fade instead of position animation.

DEPENDS ON: useAtRiskReview hook (broccoli-dashboard-479).
title: AtRiskReviewCard: draggable card with Framer Motion
